what_it_does: The prescriptive module constitution for board_edges/ — defines the file-discovered EDGE-KIND registry for NOTICEBOARD items: the row schema, the live set of edge kinds, where new edges go, the discovery seam, and prohibitions.
observations: States the registry REUSES runtime/relation_types.py:RelationTypeRegistry verbatim — one mechanism, a separate vocabulary dir.
Records a dated decision (2026-06-15, lead+fork+advisor) explaining why board_edges is a SEPARATE dir from relation_types/: structural/provenance edges vs corpus-semantic edges.
Row schema: required id (==filename) and directed(bool); optional inverse/near/far/label/desc; malformed entries RAISE at discovery.
Lists four live edge-kinds: authored_by, attached_to, sourced_from, promoted_from (with directions and targets).
Extend mechanism: drop an <id>.py file; cc_board.reset_registries() re-discovers it; cited as proven by tests/cc_board_acceptance.py via a 'blocks' edge.
Seam: discovered by runtime/cc_board.py:_edges_reg(), consumed by file_item validating each link kind fail-loud.
Never section: no inlined enum, no second registry mechanism, no silent unregistered edge-kinds.
Has an auto-reflected 'Agent-authored entries' section appended live by the create face.
standouts: Explicit 'one MECHANISM, not one dir' principle separating reuse of a class from sharing a namespace.
A deferred-unification clause: unify into relation_types/ only when the Heart's cross-registry traversal engine lands, tracked as a board idea item not dropped.
Live edge set framed as 'the drift home' kept green by acceptance tests.
conventions: Follows the vault constitution schema (frontmatter with typed relations, Is/Why/Guarantees/Seam/Never sections).
Row id must equal filename; RELATION_TYPE = {...} per-file declaration.
Cites the discovering/consuming code paths and the acceptance test that proves the row-add-no-code claim.
notes: Constitution for the noticeboard's typed edge vocabulary — same registry class as relation_types but a deliberately separate dir for structural/provenance edges.
questions: The live-set lists promoted_from but the extend example/tests mention a 'blocks' edge — is the documented live set fully in sync with the actual files?
What exactly distinguishes near/far semantics here given these are structural rather than corpus edges?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it is a constitution and it governs the board-edge vocabulary exactly as the frontmatter claims.
apparent_themes: Deliberate deferred unification with provenance tracking
Separation of structural/provenance edges from corpus-semantic edges
intention_signals: Tries to justify and govern a separate edge namespace for the board while insisting it stays on the single shared relation-registry mechanism, so the system gains a vocabulary without forking machinery.
novelty: The careful articulated distinction between sharing a mechanism (the RelationTypeRegistry class) versus sharing a directory/vocabulary, with a dated multi-party decision rationale.
contribution: Contributes the governing rules and the live edge-kind vocabulary (authored_by/attached_to/sourced_from/promoted_from) for noticeboard item links, plus the rationale for keeping that vocabulary separate.
summary_for_embedding: A prescriptive constitution defining the board_edges/ edge-kind registry for noticeboard items — its row schema, four live edge kinds, discovery seam via RelationTypeRegistry, and separation from relation_types/.
intention_for_embedding: To govern a separate typed edge vocabulary for board-item provenance/structural links while reusing the single shared relation-type registry mechanism.
